pageBufferSize.name: pageBufferSize
pageBufferSize.description: The page buffer size is used 1) when to call a fetch request and 2) provide a threshold for optimized scrolling performance on data sets at extreme scale. This performance optimization method is commonly known as virtual scrolling. A page buffer size of 2 means, 2 pages worth of rendered items will be prerended above the viewport and 2 pages will be rendered below the viewport for a total of 5 pages at any given time. If the current data set cannot sufficiently cater to the buffer needs then a fetch request will be called for the optimal data set. Page size is automatically calculated by how many items can fit within the current viewport even if the heights are dynamic. (!) It is recommended to increase the buffer size on smaller viewport, and decrease the buffer size on smaller viewport for optimal performance.

scrollTarget.description: Reference to a (parent) DOM element that is already providing the scroll container. (!) Mind that a parent element in React is mounted after the child. If passing an element via a ref, a ref callback is generally required to sync the parent mount with the child mount. Pass a DOM element as return result from a function. e.g. `() => document.querySelector('.container')` If the parent DOM is not available yet (e.g. still mounting), a sync callback is provided to finalize the InfiniteScroll mount side effects. e.g. setting initial scroll position.

{
name: 'MenuItem', : if {name} is not specified then 'MenuItem' will return as the default.
children: ,
key: '1'
}
{
name: 'MenuGroup',
items: ,

}

key: '3'
}

key : key of item,
keyPath : keyPath to item,
openKeyMap : Array of open keys,

type : Item type e.g. 'collapsible-header'

rowsData.name: rowsData
rowsData.description:
Array of rows. Each row is a select option. `MultiSelectRowData` fields:
- `id` (string | number) - Unique string/num identifier for this item. Required unless `props.rowKeyAttribute` specifies a different filed.
- `label` (string) - Text displayed in the input when selected and as the default option content in the dropdown. Use `props.rowRenderer` to customize option appearance.
- `subLabel` (string) - Secondary text displayed below the option label in the dropdown. Use `props.rowRenderer` to customize option appearance.
- `multiSelectedItemTagProps` ([MultiSelectedItemTagProps](#!/MultiSelectedItemTag)) - Additional properties passed to the tag component for each selected item.
- `disabled` (boolean) - Disables selection of the option. Only styled for default rendering; with custom `props.rowRenderer`, disabled styling is the consumer's responsibility.
- `rowProps` ([FlexLayoutProps](#!/FlexLayout)) - Additional properties passed to the option's DOM element.
- `checkboxProps` ([CheckboxProps](#!/Checkbox)) - Additional properties passed to the Checkbox.
- `nestedRows` (MultiSelectRowData[]) - Nested selection. Only a single level of nesting is supported.

placement.name: placement
placement.description:
The placement position of the tooltip based on the given target selector element. Default is 'center'
TutorialTipPlacement.CENTER - center of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.TOP - top of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.TOP_LEFT - top left corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.TOP_RIGHT - top right corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.LEFT - left of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.RIGHT - right of the targetSelector container TutorialTipPlacement.BOTTOM - bottom of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.BOTTOM_LEFT - bottom left corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.BOTTOM_RIGHT - bottom right corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.LEFT_BOTTOM - left bottom corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.RIGHT_BOTTOM - right bottom corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.LEFT_TOP - left top corner of the targetSelector container
TutorialTipPlacement.RIGHT_TOP - right top corner of the targetSelector container
